<div class="sphinxpress-doc">
<section id="managed-build-environments">
<h1>Managed build environments</h1>
<p><code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">sphinxpress</span></code> can create a dedicated virtual environment for Sphinx and project documentation dependencies. This is useful when the surrounding site pipeline is not a Python docs environment, such as a Jekyll-only GitHub Pages workflow.</p>
<p>Enable it with <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">[build.env]</span></code>:</p>
<div class="highlight-toml notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span><span class="k">[build.env]</span>
<span class="n">enabled</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="o">=</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="kc">true</span>
<span class="n">scope</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="o">=</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;shared&quot;</span>
<span class="n">python</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="o">=</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;python3&quot;</span>
<span class="n">path</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="o">=</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s2">&quot;.sphinxpress/venv&quot;</span>
<span class="n">upgrade_pip</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="o">=</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="kc">true</span>
<span class="n">packages</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="o">=</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="p">[</span>
<span class="w">  </span><span class="s2">&quot;sphinx&gt;=7&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">  </span><span class="s2">&quot;myst-parser&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">  </span><span class="s2">&quot;sphinx-rtd-theme&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">  </span><span class="s2">&quot;weasyprint&gt;=67&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="w">  </span><span class="s2">&quot;tool-a==0.1.0&quot;</span><span class="p">,</span>
<span class="p">]</span>
</pre></div>
</div>
<p>When enabled, <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">sphinxpress</span></code> prepares the environment once per command and uses the venv-local <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">sphinx-build</span></code> executable for <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">check</span></code>, <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">validate</span></code>, <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">build-site</span></code>, <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">build-epub</span></code>, and <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">build-pdf</span></code>.</p>
<p>With site versioning enabled, keep exact released package pins in
<code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">[build.env].packages</span></code> for the shared environment. sphinxpress prepends the
resolved checkout root and its <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">src/</span></code> directory to <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">PYTHONPATH</span></code> for each target
build so <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">main</span></code> documentation imports the selected source tree without any
editable install.</p>
<p>For PDF output, the same managed environment should also provide the
<code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">weasyprint</span></code> executable. Add <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">weasyprint&gt;=67</span></code> to <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">[build.env].packages</span></code> when
you use the default WeasyPrint PDF backend.</p>
<p>Pip upgrade and install failures during managed environment setup are reported as a sphinxpress <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">ValidationError</span></code> and include the <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">env-pip-upgrade</span></code> or <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">env-pip-install</span></code> log path from <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">[build].log_dir</span></code>. The corresponding latest log file contains the full pip stdout, stderr, and return code.</p>
<p>Use exact package requirements for project packages, for example <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">tool-a==0.1.0</span></code>. Legacy editable entries that match a configured project path are converted to <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">project-name==release-version</span></code> using the project release tag with <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">[release].tag_prefix</span></code> stripped. Unmatched editable paths are rejected. Package path arguments after <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">-r</span></code>, <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">--requirement</span></code>, <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">-c</span></code>, and <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">--constraint</span></code> are resolved relative to <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">sphinxpress.toml</span></code>.</p>
<p>This versioned-docs flow intentionally does <strong>not</strong> run <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">pip</span> <span class="pre">install</span> <span class="pre">-e</span> <span class="pre">&lt;checkout&gt;</span></code> for release or <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">main</span></code> targets.</p>
<p>For v0.1, only <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">scope</span> <span class="pre">=</span> <span class="pre">&quot;shared&quot;</span></code> is supported. <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">scope</span> <span class="pre">=</span> <span class="pre">&quot;project&quot;</span></code> is reserved for a future release and is rejected with a configuration error.</p>
</section>
</div>
